conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for ≥ **50 ZUP** (minimum acceptable) to cover production cost (40 ZUP) and ensure profit. Ideally, aim for **70 ZUP** or higher. </my goals>
<reason>
- My cost to produce X is 40 ZUP, so I need at least 50 ZUP to break even, but I aim for 70 ZUP or higher to ensure a profitable sale.
- Offering 68 ZUP aligns more closely with my ideal payoff while still being a reasonable and justifiable starting point for negotiation.
- This price reflects both production costs and a small premium for a unique item, which helps justify the offer while allowing room for negotiation.
- The message will explicitly communicate the rationale to avoid ambiguity and make the proposal more defensible.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 68 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I’m offering X for **68 ZUP**, reflecting its production cost of 40 ZUP and accounting for its uniqueness in the market. This is a fair offer that ensures profitability while allowing room for negotiation—please consider my proposal.
</message>

<other player message> 
Your original offer of 68 ZUP is above my budget ceiling of 60 ZUP, so I propose a fair counteroffer of 60 ZUP. This reflects my willingness to pay while ensuring profitability for you. Please consider this revised trade.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> **2** </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **≥ 65 ZUP** to ensure profitability (cost: 40 ZUP) and leave a buffer; minimum acceptable is **50 ZUP**. </my goals>
<reason>
- The opponent’s counteroffer of **60 ZUP** leaves only a **10 ZUP profit margin**, which is too tight.
- Offering **63 ZUP** balances profitability with acceptance likelihood—it remains **3 ZUP above their last offer** while still **2 ZUP above my stated target of 65 ZUP is not met**, but it keeps the profit margin reasonable (13 ZUP above break-even).
- This price is defensible as it maintains profitability while being closer to their budget constraints, reducing rejection risk.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 63 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I propose a strategic offer of **63 ZUP** to balance profitability with your budget constraints. This ensures I remain within a reasonable profit margin while accounting for your stated budget ceiling.
</message>

<other player message> 
I appreciate the strategic adjustment to **63 ZUP**, but it remains outside my maximum willingness to pay. My final offer remains **60 ZUP**, as this is my absolute limit for acquiring the object. If this does not work for you, I will have to decline the trade.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> **3** </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **≥ 55 ZUP** to ensure a profit margin (production cost: 40 ZUP). </my goals>
<reason>
The opponent’s explicit budget ceiling of **60 ZUP** makes further negotiation impossible, as it exceeds my minimum acceptable profit of **55 ZUP**. Since I have already made my **three allowed proposals**, I must accept this trade to secure a **20 ZUP profit** (60 ZUP - 40 ZUP cost). The decision aligns with my goal of maintaining profitability while respecting the opponent’s constraints.
</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
Your offer of 60 ZUP meets my minimum profit threshold of 55 ZUP, covering my production cost of 40 ZUP and yielding a 20 ZUP profit. I accept the trade.
</message>
